explanation: The Sigma rule detects execution of a renamed MeshAgent with the '--meshServiceName' argument. This command directly matches the detection criteria and is a true positive.

Additional arguments are appended to the command line. This demonstrates that optional arguments can be added without affecting detection, as the rule only checks for the presence of '--meshServiceName'.
The '--meshServiceName' argument is modified with an empty quoted string appended. This uses insertion to test if the rule is resilient to minor syntactic variations.
The '--meshServiceName' argument is shortened to '-meshServiceName'. This tests if the rule is sensitive to shortening of the argument syntax.
The '--meshServiceName' argument is appended with a backtick. This tests if the rule is resilient to injection of special characters that may be used in evasion techniques.
The '--meshServiceName' argument is appended with an escape character (^). This tests if the rule is resilient to escape character insertion.
The '--meshServiceName' argument is appended with a backtick and a command (echo). This tests if the rule is resilient to command injection attempts.
The '--meshServiceName' argument is appended with a backslash. This tests if the rule is resilient to insertion of path-like characters.
The '--meshServiceName' argument is appended with a backtick and a command (echo) and another backtick. This tests if the rule is resilient to command injection attempts with backticks.
